sentence: She was ______ by her grandparents.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring up> significa criar a un niño.
tip: Grandparents did this — they raised the child.

sentence: Let's ______ with the meeting.
explanation: El phrasal verb <carry on> significa continuar.
tip: Don't stop — keep going.

sentence: We need to ______ at the hotel before 3 PM.
explanation: El phrasal verb <check in> significa registrarse.
tip: You do this at a hotel reception when you arrive.

sentence: I just ______ that she moved to London.
explanation: El phrasal verb <find out> significa descubrir.
tip: She learned new information — she discovered it.

sentence: She ______ well with all her classmates.
explanation: El phrasal verb <get along> significa llevarse bien.
tip: She has a good relationship with her classmates.

sentence: Please ______ at the next bus stop.
explanation: El phrasal verb <get off> significa bajarse del transporte.
tip: You do this when the bus reaches your stop.

sentence: Don't ______! You can do it!
explanation: El phrasal verb <give up> significa rendirse.
tip: Don't stop trying!

sentence: He ______ in a small village in Spain.
explanation: El phrasal verb <grow up> significa crecer.
tip: He became an adult in a small village.

sentence: We like to ______ at the park on weekends.
explanation: El phrasal verb <hang out> significa pasar el rato.
tip: You do this with friends when you have free time.

sentence: ______! We're going to be late!
explanation: El phrasal verb <hurry up> significa darse prisa.
tip: Be faster! Don't be slow!

sentence: You need to ______ with your username and password.
explanation: El phrasal verb <log in> significa iniciar sesión.
tip: You do this on a computer before using it.

sentence: They ______ to their new apartment last week.
explanation: El phrasal verb <move in> significa mudarse a un lugar.
tip: They started living in a new place.

sentence: We've ______ milk. Can you buy some?
explanation: El phrasal verb <run out of> significa quedarse sin algo.
tip: There's no more milk — it's finished.

sentence: Can you help me ______ the new computer?
explanation: El phrasal verb <set up> significa instalar u organizar.
tip: You do this with new equipment — prepare it for use.

sentence: He didn't ______ to the party last night.
explanation: El phrasal verb <show up> significa aparecer o presentarse.
tip: He didn't arrive — he didn't appear.

sentence: Please ______. You're driving too fast.
explanation: El phrasal verb <slow down> significa reducir velocidad.
tip: The opposite of speeding up.

sentence: We need to ______ this problem quickly.
explanation: El phrasal verb <sort out> significa resolver.
tip: You do this with a problem — you fix it.

sentence: Can I ______ this dress before buying it?
explanation: El phrasal verb <try on> significa probarse ropa.
tip: You do this in a shop with clothes before buying.

sentence: Please ______ after the party.
explanation: El phrasal verb <clean up> significa limpiar.
tip: You do this after a party — make everything tidy.

sentence: Don't forget to ______ the form completely.
explanation: El phrasal verb <fill in> significa rellenar un formulario.
tip: You do this with a form — write the information.

sentence: We need to ______ of the hotel by noon.
explanation: El phrasal verb <check out> significa pagar e irse del hotel.
tip: You do this at a hotel when you leave.

sentence: Don't forget to ______ before closing the computer.
explanation: El phrasal verb <log out> significa cerrar sesión.
tip: You do this on a computer when you finish working.

sentence: She ______ of her old apartment last month.
explanation: El phrasal verb <move out> significa mudarse de un lugar.
tip: She left her old apartment — she no longer lives there.

sentence: Can you ______ the trash, please?
explanation: El phrasal verb <take out> significa sacar.
tip: You do this with garbage — bring it outside.

sentence: He needs to ______ his room before his parents arrive.
explanation: El phrasal verb <clean up> significa limpiar y ordenar.
tip: Make the room tidy before the guests arrive.
